stat_of_week: 19 HR - BaseballPro77 set a new single-week team home run record, with Trout (5), Murakami (4), and Riley (3) leading a power surge that obliterated Wisconsin Rapids in a 7-2 rout
thriller_note: A dead-even tie where neither team could separate itself in the pitching categories left both squads frustrated — the Wombats needed a win to climb out of the basement while the Roughnecks let a winnable matchup slip into a split, and both now face must-perform weeks to stay relevant.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 didn't just win this week — they made history, setting the new single-week HR record at 19 while posting a 2.33 ERA and going 7-2 against Wisconsin Rapids. Mike Trout (9 R, 5 HR, 9 RBI), Munetaka Murakami (4 HR, 9 RBI), and Austin Riley (3 HR, 9 RBI) formed arguably the most destructive trio of any single week this season. With a loaded lineup and a suddenly reliable rotation behind Gavin Williams and George Kirby, BaseballPro77 is making a serious case to be considered the league's best team.
lucky_reason: Despite posting only 4 HR, a 4.40 ERA, and the second-fewest strikeouts of any team this week, the Benchwarmers somehow won 2 of their 10 categories — their +1.21 luck delta remains the most unsustainable number in the league.
unlucky_reason: The Twins won their matchup 6-4 but their luck delta of -0.61 is the worst in the league, meaning their category output consistently outpaces their actual results — they are producing like a top-five team and being rewarded like an eighth-place one.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 set two new single-week records: most HR by a team with 19 (previous: unknown prior mark, now established) and most RBI by a team with 39, both in Week 4 — the previous HR and RBI records are now jointly held by BaseballPro77 themselves.
power_rankings: 1. BaseballPro77 | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI week with a 2.33 ERA confirms this is the most complete roster in the league right now — Trout, Murakami, and Riley are a top-three power trio in the game | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Still the most consistent team over four weeks with category wins totals of 9, 8, 8, and 4 — one off week doesn't erase the best overall resume in the league, and Weathers (18 K) reminded everyone the staff has weapons | same

3. General Hospital | An 8-2 demolition powered by Will Warren (17 K, 1.69 ERA), league-leading 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA — this rotation is the best in the league and Kumar Rocker hasn't even thrown a pitch yet | up

4. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Paul Skenes (0.90 ERA, 0.50 WHIP), Imanaga, and Framber Valdez combined for a filthy 8 QS and a 2.49 ERA — the pitching staff is genuinely elite and the offense is starting to show up around it | up

5. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge (5 HR), James Wood, and José Caballero anchored another strong offensive week, and the rotation finally delivered a 3.01 ERA with 5 QS — this feels like the team clicking into gear | same

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Won 5-4 with Byron Buxton (3 HR) and a balanced lineup carrying the load — four waiver adds show an aggressive front office, and the 8 category wins in Week 5 history suggests a hot streak may be brewing | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR) is one of the most dangerous one-two punches in the league, and Reid Detmers' 1.29 ERA hints at a rotation stabilizing | up

8. Hiroshima Radiation | Tarik Skubal and Nathan Eovaldi both posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA was a sparkling 2.81, but a -1 NSVH tanked the pitching side and cost them the matchup — the saves situation is a real problem | down

9. North Correa | Lost 5-2 despite Chris Sale (0.86 WHIP) and solid contributions from Nico Hoerner (11 RBI) — the pitching ERA of 5.56 is the culprit, and Austin Wells replacing Realmuto is a meaningful upgrade worth watching | same

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A 4-4 tie when a win was there for the taking is the story — Jacob Misiorowski (14 K) and Raisel Iglesias (3 NSVH) showed real weapons, but the offense left runs on the table all week | same

11. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong were bright spots, but a 4.35 ERA, only 3 QS, and a .283 OBP won't win matchups — adding JR Ritchie while dropping Sasaki is a puzzling rotation decision | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero (3 HR) had a strong week but MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 4.09 ERA tanked the pitching categories — six category wins added to Week 5's history suggests a bounce-back may be coming | up

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got historically embarrassed by BaseballPro77's record-setting week, but Bryan Woo (2 QS) and Mason Miller (4 NSVH, 0.50 WHIP) showed this team has real relief-side assets to build around | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King (0.82 ERA) was outstanding and Cody Bellinger (.481 OBP) continues to be productive, but 4 HR and only 45 hits in a 10-category format means an 8-2 drubbing was almost inevitable — the roster needs a power injection badly | down
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Noelvi Marte, Josh Jung; dropped Sam Antonacci, Jonathan Loáisiga | Four adds in one week is aggressive roster management — Winn and Garrett give rotation depth the Bombers desperately needed, while Marte and Jung patch the corner infield, though dropping Loáisiga removes a useful late-inning arm.

General Hospital | A | Added Kumar Rocker; dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk | This is the move of the week — Rocker slots into an already elite rotation that posted 6 QS and a 2.68 ERA, and if he delivers even half of his upside, General Hospital's staff becomes untouchable.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | C | Added Chase Dollander, Justin Bruihl, Erik Miller, Ildemaro Vargas; dropped Jack Flaherty, Justin Sterner, Taylor Clarke, Matt Strahm | Losing Strahm (who immediately landed on BaseballPro77) stings, and Flaherty had obvious upside — Dollander is a high-ceiling flier but this is a lot of turnover for a team already dealing with bad luck.

Canberra Wombats | D | Added JR Ritchie; dropped Roki Sasaki | Dropping Sasaki — a legitimate ace-caliber arm — for JR Ritchie is a head-scratcher of the highest order; if Sasaki gets picked up by a contender, this could be the worst transaction of the season.

North Correa | B | Added Austin Wells; dropped J.T. Realmuto | A straightforward upgrade at catcher — Wells has better counting stats upside and Realmuto has been a liability all season; a clean, smart move from a manager who clearly identified a positional weakness.

Riverside Roughnecks | B | Added Sam Bachman, Victor Vodnik; dropped Mark Vientos | Doubling down on bullpen depth makes sense given the team's saves-and-holds structure, and Bachman in particular has closing upside — losing Vientos hurts positional flexibility but the saves are a priority.

Benchwarmers | D | Added Casey Schmitt; dropped Josh Naylor, Max Scherzer, Javier Assad | Dropping Scherzer and Assad simultaneously guts whatever rotation depth the Benchwarmers had, and adding Schmitt doesn't come close to replacing the lost pitching — this looks like a team without a plan heading into a crucial stretch.
